texto: Monotheism generally allows for no greys. Ideas are either true or false. Hence, although science develops out of the alchemy of the medieval Christian milieu (derived from Arabic alchemy, which was stimulated by the much earlier Chinese alchemy), science is not understood by the nonscientific monotheistic population. The general Western public mistakenly thinks science presents unalterable truth, as does their religion, rather than theories to be tested and continually discarded to be replaced by new hypotheses, which is the actual scientific method.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jordan D. Paper sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “Monotheism generally allows for no greys. Ideas are either true or false. Hence, although science…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, christianity, science, útil em momentos de science e scientific-method.
